_meta.method: Targeted web research of major Taiwanese Chan/Zen institutions, cross-referenced with official sites and Wikipedia. Taiwanese Chan treated as Zen for taxonomy.
_meta.coverageEstimate: High for the four major Chan institutions (Dharma Drum, Chung Tai, Fo Guang Shan, Linji-Rinzai). Lower for smaller independent zendos and Sanbo Zen affiliates.
knownGaps: Hundreds of small Chung Tai branch meditation centers across Taiwan not enumerated.
Numerous Fo Guang Shan branch temples with Chan halls not enumerated.
Smaller independent Chan groups, Sanbo Zen affiliates and Yuanjue Hall not separately verified.

places.name: Dharma Drum Mountain World Center for Buddhist Education
places.address: No. 555, Fagu Rd., Jinshan District, New Taipei City 20842, Taiwan
places.notes: International headquarters and main monastery of the Dharma Drum Mountain organization founded by Chan Master Sheng-yen; one of the Four Great Mountains of Taiwanese Buddhism.

places.name: Nung Chan Monastery (Water-Moon Dharma Center)
places.address: No. 89, Lane 65, Daye Rd., Beitou District, Taipei City 112, Taiwan
places.notes: Principal Taipei branch of Dharma Drum Mountain, founded 1975 by Master Dongchu; site of Sheng-yen's early teaching. Famed Water-Moon Dharma Center hall.

places.name: Chung Tai Chan Monastery
places.address: No. 2, Zhongtai Rd., Puli Township, Nantou County 545, Taiwan
places.notes: Main monastery of Chung Tai Shan (Linji Chan), founded by Wei Chueh in 1987; 136 m tall, parent of 108 affiliated Chan meditation centers.

places.name: Fo Guang Shan Monastery (Chan Hall)
places.lineage: Chan / Linji (Fo Guang Shan, Humanistic Buddhism)
places.address: No. 153, Xingtian Rd., Dashu District, Kaohsiung City 840, Taiwan
places.notes: Headquarters of Fo Guang Shan, founded 1967 by Hsing Yun; Chan Hall behind Main Shrine on third floor of Tathagata Building, capacity 400+.

places.name: Linji Huguo Chan Temple
places.address: No. 9, Lane 27, Yuluen St., Zhongshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan
places.notes: Historic Japanese-built Rinzai temple (1900-1911), now affiliated with Fo Guang Shan; weekly Sunday Chan meditation sessions.

places.name: Dharma Drum Retreat Center (Sanyi Branch)
places.notes: Dharma Drum branch retreat / DILA campus; one of multiple DDM education centers across Taiwan.
